portwatch_chokepoints.note: Daily AIS-derived tanker counts. Anomaly = 7-day mean below threshold vs pre-crisis baseline OR 30d avg (ports avg>=5). T6-S45: 7d mean (was single-day, smooths ~5d feed lag) + strategic ports alert at -40% (0.60), others -50% (0.50).

items.title: Fuel Refining Margins Hit Record Highs as Markets Tighten
items.summary: Refining margins for gasoline and diesel jumped this week to new record highs after the re-escalation in the Middle East, Russia’s ban on diesel exports, and crumbling global fuel inventories. The surge in fuel margins and the price spread over cr...

items.title: More LNG Carriers Brave the Strait of Hormuz Despite Renewed Hostilities
items.summary: Close to half a dozen liquefied natural gas carriers have gone into the Strait of Hormuz in the past few days and six have exited the strait, despite the recent return to hostilities between the United States and Iran. According to Kpler data cite...

items.title: India Expands Strategic Oil Reserves With New ONGC Storage Plan
items.summary: India’s ONGC plans to build up a strategic national reserve of crude totalling 1.75 million tons, or 12.8 million barrels. The company would also ask the government to allow the commercial use of the stockpile, Reuters reported. India currently ha...

items.title: Oil Prices Set for Weekly Gain as Hormuz Tensions Escalate
items.summary: Crude oil prices were on course for a weekly gain today as hostilities between the United States and Iran flared up again earlier in the week. At the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $76.60 per barrel, with West Texas Intermediate at $7...

items.title: The Coming Power War That Will Define the AI Era
items.summary: Every great economic era has been defined by a fight over a single resource. In the 19th century, it was coal, and the British Empire was built on top of it. In the 20th century, it was oil, and the modern Middle East and American postwar dominanc...

items.title: People Are Talking About Contango While Oil Markets Are Far From Recovered
items.summary: While financial desks obsess over paper structures and Brent crude briefly flirted with contango, the broader market is missing the forest for the trees. Brent crude’s recent rebound from $71 to $79 a barrel is not merely a brief short-covering bo...

items.title: The Strait Of Malacca Faces Growing Fears Of Copycat Shipping Fees
items.summary: Oil prices spiked again on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump declared that the ceasefire deal with Iran is over and vowed to strike Iran again, effectively ending the tentative peace deal barely a month after signing. Iran struck three c...

items.title: Fed Sees Oil Prices Cooling Despite Renewed Iran War
items.summary: The Federal Reserve still thinks energy prices are headed lower even as missiles are flying again in the Middle East. New York Fed President John Williams said Thursday that the market's expectation for oil prices to ease over the next six to 12 m...

items.title: Turkey and Iraq Move to Keep Critical Oil Export Route Alive
items.summary: Iraq just bought itself another year. Turkey and Iraq are expected to sign a 12-month extension of the agreement governing the Iraq-Turkey crude oil pipeline within days, preventing a July 27 expiration that threatened one of Baghdad's few remaini...

items.title: Washington Draws a Line for Iraq’s New Leader
items.summary: Iraq’s new prime minister, Ali Al-Zaidi, will meet U.S. President Donald Trump in Washington, D.C. on 14 July - shortly after America’s Independence Day on July 4th. Trump will no doubt highlight America’s struggle for independence and will press...

items.title: U.S. Oil Dominance Keeps Growing Despite Lower Prices
items.summary: While much of the world spent 2025 debating peak oil, the United States was busy setting another production record. New data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration showed that U.S. crude oil production, including lease condensate, average...

items.title: Iran War May Ignite Middle East Market Share Race, But Not Yet
items.summary: When the UAE said it was leaving OPEC so it could boost production as much as it wanted, it sparked speculation, yet again, that OPEC was falling apart. The peace deal between the United States and Iran was a done deal in the eyes of oil traders,...

comunicacoes.label: Comunicacoes
comunicacoes.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: 1 de 4+ indicadores presentes.

defesa.label: Defesa
defesa.note: Tema severamente sub-instrumentado: incendios e o gap mais critico (PT/Norte alto risco).

themes.note: Decomposicao em 8 temas da enciclopedia (T6-S26). Score=None quando dados insuficientes. Anti-synthetic: nao fabrica scores. Cada tema lista gaps_to_close_priority com APIs livres a integrar em S27+.
